original_abstract:
Hashing has become a staple for similarity search in multimedia data because of its high query speeds and low storage requirements. In recent years, researchers have focused on multimodal hashing to facilitate cross-view searches, such as retrieving images using text tags. Because semantic labels are often available in real-world scenarios, supervised multimodal hashing (SMH) methods have been developed to leverage this information to boost retrieval accuracy. However, most existing SMH techniques suffer from high training time complexity—often scaling quadratically with the number of data points—rendering them impractical for truly large-scale datasets.

In this paper, we introduce a novel SMH method called Semantic Correlation Maximization (SCM) designed specifically for large-scale data modeling. SCM seamlessly integrates semantic labels into the hashing learning process while maintaining a linear time complexity relative to the training set size. By avoiding the explicit construction of massive pairwise similarity matrices, SCM remains highly scalable without sacrificing the depth of supervision. We also propose a sequential learning strategy to solve for the hash functions bit-by-bit with closed-form solutions, eliminating the need for tedious hyper-parameter tuning. Extensive experiments on two real-world datasets demonstrate that SCM significantly outperforms state-of-the-art SMH methods in both search accuracy and computational efficiency.
humanized_abstract:
Hashing has been widely used for similarity search in multimedia data due to its efficiency in search speed and storage capacity. Recently, multimodal hashing for cross-view search has attracted more and more attention, such as image search with text tags. While many semantic labels are available in many practical applications, the supervised multimodal hashing (SMH) methods have been proposed to further improve the search performance by fully utilizing the labels. Unfortunately, most of the existing SMH methods suffer from a high time complexity of O(n2), which limits their applicability to large-scale data sets.

In this paper, we propose a novel SMH model called Semantic Correlation Maximization (SCM) for large-scale data modeling. By incorporating the semantic labels into the hashing learning process with linear time complexity to the size of the training data, SCM excludes the explicit computation of the large pairsim matrices. To solve the optimization problem in the proposed SCM framework, we develop a sequential learning algorithm to successively solve for the hash functions by exploiting the closed form solutions and reduce the large number of hyper-parameter tunings. The experimental results show that SCM outperforms the state-of-the-art SMH methods on two real-world large-scale datasets in terms of both search precision and efficiency.

In this paper, we propose a novel SMH model called Semantic Correlation Maximization (SCM) for large-scale data modeling.
